title: Design-by-Contract Guards for Shared Skills
summary: Shared skills hand-copy data shapes (the tasks JSON format especially) inline across many producers and consumers, causing schema drift, and prose 'STOP if invalid' guards are soft enough that LLMs rationalize past them. The plan adds Design-by-Contract + Fail-Fast guards: each shared format is centralized into one real JSON Schema in a new top-level contracts/ folder, validated by a single python3+jsonschema validate-schema.sh script invoked as a literal bash Step-0 inside each skill's `## Contract` section. Rollout is a big-bang across all three format waists (tasks, seed, runner-result) with strict schemas and a required schema_version. register-skill gains a conditional script-backed gate, and verification combines per-skill malformed-input evals with one deterministic validator test.
decisions: Tier 1 (file exists) and Tier 2 (JSON shape validates) entry checks are script-backed; prose-only 'STOP if invalid' is rejected as soft. Tier 3 (semantic) stays prose-only because it cannot be scripted.
Skills whose input is freeform prose (to-seed from conversation, grill-me) are exempt from Tier 1/2 checks; script-backed guards apply only where input is a structured file.
One shared validator script lives in claude-code-shared/scripts/, backed by one canonical JSON Schema file per format. Not per-skill bespoke validators.

Data-shape rules live in ONE schema file per format. Each skill's `## Contract` section is short: it declares which schema it produces/consumes plus any skill-specific invariants, and never hand-copies the shape.
A new first-class claude-code-shared/contracts/ directory holds all three format pairs (schema.json + contract.md), alongside skills/, scripts/, agents/, resources/.

The validator primitive is a literal copy-pasteable bash Step-0 inside the `## Contract` section: `validate-schema.sh <schema-path> <input-path>`, exit 0 pass / non-zero stop, with 'on non-zero, STOP and report stderr, do not proceed'. Not hook-based: there is no clean hook trigger for 'skill about to consume file Y'.

Accepted limitation: the validator trigger is itself prose, so enforcement is harder, not hard. The check is deterministic when run; the residual risk that the agent skips Step-0 is policed by improve-skill evals, not eliminated.

The validator engine is python3 + the jsonschema library (already installed, 4.25.1, Draft 2020-12 capable). It enforces the full schema (types, enums, nesting, additionalProperties), not just required-key presence.

On missing `import jsonschema`, validate-schema.sh hard-fails: prints `pip install jsonschema` and exits non-zero. It never auto-installs and never silently degrades to key-only checks. The dotfiles bootstrap owns installing jsonschema for fresh machines.
Rollout is big-bang across all three format waists (tasks + seed + runner-result) in one pass, not a tracer bullet, justified by the small measured blast radius on the tasks waist.
All schemas are strict: `additionalProperties: false`, with known-legitimate optional fields enumerated explicitly.
Quirk cleanup in the same pass: cull `source_branch` from to-e2e-tasks (no consumer reads it); promote `linear_url` (written back by tasks-to-linear) to a documented optional output field.

`schema_version` is required and enforced as a JSON Schema `const` on all three formats (tasks '1', seed already '2', runner-result gains '1'). A version-less in-flight file hitting a versioned consumer should STOP loudly, which is correct Fail-Fast behavior.

register-skill gains a conditional, script-backed gate: it greps a SKILL.md for strong format signals (docs/tasks/, docs/seeds/, *-schema.json, contracts/) and hard-FAILs registration if such a signal is present without a `## Contract` section carrying a validate-schema.sh line. Skills touching no shared format are unaffected.

The register-skill gate ships as hard-FAIL from day one with no WARN phase, because the big-bang migrates every current format-touching skill with its `## Contract` in the same pass, leaving no legacy skill to trip a false failure.

Verification has two distinct legs: a malformed-input eval scenario per format-consuming skill (asserts the skill STOPs with a clear error, policing the skip risk), plus one deterministic fixtures test for validate-schema.sh itself.

The single validator test is a deliberate carve-out from the standing 'no unit tests for infra' rule, because it is the one load-bearing guard the whole architecture rests on and an LLM eval cannot prove a bash script actually rejects bad JSON.
grill-me / grill-with-docs overlap is out of scope; both are freeform-input skills touching no shared format, and deduping them is a separate refactor.
next_action: Run /to-tasks to break this into a big-bang migration task breakdown
problem_statement: Shared skills hand-copy data shapes inline across producers and consumers, causing schema drift. The tasks JSON format has four producers each re-declaring the shape in their own SKILL.md. Prose 'STOP if invalid' guards are soft and get rationalized past under load. There is no machine-checkable contract enforcement, and the only existing 'validator' (extract-prd-json.sh) checks just required-key presence, not the real schema.
solution: Introduce Design-by-Contract + Fail-Fast guards backed by real JSON Schemas. Centralize each shared format into one schema in a new contracts/ folder, enforce via a single python3+jsonschema validate-schema.sh invoked as a literal bash Step-0 in each skill's `## Contract` section, gate registration on contract presence for format-touching skills, and verify with per-skill malformed-input evals plus one deterministic validator test.
evidence: Audit found four task-format producers (to-tasks, to-e2e-tasks, debug, improve-component) each hand-copying the shape inline, with the only working validator (extract-prd-json.sh) checking only required-key presence. This drift was flagged the highest-priority problem. Blast-radius sampling showed the four producers are otherwise near-uniform: the only divergences are source_branch (e2e-only, unused by any consumer) and linear_url (a tasks-to-linear retrofit), and no producer emits schema_version.
success_metrics: Each shared format is defined exactly once; no SKILL.md hand-copies a data shape
validate-schema.sh rejects every malformed fixture (missing field, wrong schema_version, extra field) and passes a valid file
Every format-consuming skill STOPs with a clear error on malformed input in its improve-skill eval scenario
register-skill hard-fails any new format-touching skill that lacks a `## Contract` section
implementation_decisions: New directory claude-code-shared/contracts/ holding three pairs: task-schema.json + task-contract.md, seed-schema.json + seed-contract.md, runner-result-schema.json + runner-result-contract.md

task-schema.json authored from the existing prose resources/task-schema.md; seed-schema.json promoted from to-prd-html/resources/prd-data-schema.json; runner-result-schema.json moved from resources/ with runner-result-contract.md newly authored (it does not exist today)
New script claude-code-shared/scripts/validate-schema.sh <schema-path> <instance-path>, python3+jsonschema backed, exit 0 valid / non-zero invalid with clear stderr
Tasks strict schema: additionalProperties:false; schema_version required as const '1'; browser_verify optional; linear_url optional (promoted); source_branch dropped
Each format-touching SKILL.md gains a `## Contract` section with a literal bash Step-0 validate-schema.sh invocation and a 'STOP on non-zero exit' instruction

All four task producers (to-tasks, to-e2e-tasks, debug, improve-component) and three consumers (run-tasks, run-task-followups, tasks-to-linear) migrated in the big-bang pass; seed and runner-result waists migrated in the same pass after sampling their consumers
register-skill updated with the conditional grep-based gate matching strong format signals only
jsonschema added to the dotfiles bootstrap install so fresh machines never hit the missing-import hard-fail
testing_decisions: One deterministic fixtures test for validate-schema.sh (valid passes; missing-field, wrong-version, extra-field all fail) as the single deliberate carve-out from the no-infra-tests rule
A malformed-input eval scenario per format-consuming skill in the improve-skill harness, asserting the skill STOPs and surfaces the validator error
Understood split: evals prove the SKILL.md prose reliably triggers validate-and-stop (via simulation); the script test proves the validator engine actually rejects bad input

out_of_scope.rationale: Separate refactor unrelated to contracts work.
Cannot be scripted; remain prose-only by design.

out_of_scope.text: Hook-based hard enforcement of the validator
out_of_scope.rationale: No clean hook trigger exists for the right event.

risks_and_tradeoffs: The validate-schema.sh trigger is prose, so an agent can still skip Step-0 entirely; mitigated by evals, not eliminated. Enforcement is harder, not hard.
Required schema_version breaks un-versioned in-flight task files in other repos on next run-tasks; accepted as correct Fail-Fast, near-empty blast (single user, no task files in this repo)
jsonschema is a pip dependency, not stdlib; a fresh machine without the bootstrap step hard-fails until it is installed
The register-skill grep gate risks false positives on skills that merely mention docs/tasks/ in prose; mitigated by matching strong signals only
Seed and runner-result waists were not blast-tested like tasks; their consumers must be sampled before authoring their strict schemas
further_notes: Tooling present on this machine: python3 3.9.6 + jsonschema 4.25.1, jq 1.7.1, node v22. check-jsonschema and ajv-cli are absent.
yajsv (Go single static binary) is the fallback swap if a zero-runtime-dependency validator is ever required
extract-prd-json.sh is the only working validator today and only checks required-key presence; it is effectively superseded by validate-schema.sh
Tasks waist map: producers to-tasks, to-e2e-tasks, debug, improve-component; consumers run-tasks, run-task-followups, tasks-to-linear; desired future producers prototype and code-review
Carried from the prior grill session (handoff 20260602-2357): Q1-Q4 resolved the mechanism, single-validator, contracts-attach-to-format, and contracts/ location decisions
